mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or CPIAUCSL remains below 3.5% for 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 70% of cases, or the correlation between the proxies and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 100k) signals rising wage pressures that push equity valuations higher through improved corporate profit margins.
failure_condition: The combined signal of UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 100k fails to generate positive 5d returns in > 50% of cases, or wage growth metrics diverge from equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 5d SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the rolling 3-month change in INDPRO drops below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5%) combined with low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 3%) creates stagflationary risks that depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of high inflation and low rates fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or the correlation between the proxies and SPX returns drops below -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Declining home construction (HOUST < 1500k) signals weak demand for housing, which negatively impacts related sectors and drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to correlate with negative 60d SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or housing starts recover above 1500k for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS rising > 0.5% in 3 months) increase discount rates and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising rates, or the FEDFUNDS increase fails to correlate with SPX returns below -0.15.
